message:
fix(core): document fleet_pane_infos activity_counter cursor store mismatch

fleet_pane_infos_from_registry reads activity_counter from
PaneRegistry's cursor map, which is not the same as the runtime's
capture pipeline cursors. In production, PaneRegistry cursors may
be empty, so activity_counter defaults to 0 for all panes.

This has no practical effect today (warm_bytes are also 0 since
TieredScrollback is not stored in the runtime), but will become
incorrect when real scrollback eviction is wired in. Added a NOTE
comment to flag this for the TieredScrollback integration pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3246,6 +3246,11 @@ fn build_fleet_pressure_signals(
     }
 }
 
+// NOTE: `activity_counter` below reads from PaneRegistry's own cursor map,
+// which may not reflect the runtime's capture pipeline cursors. When
+// TieredScrollback is wired in, this should be refactored to accept the
+// runtime's `cursors: &HashMap<u64, PaneCursor>` parameter so the fleet
+// coordinator sees real output-sequence progress.
 fn fleet_pane_infos_from_registry(registry: &PaneRegistry) -> Vec<PaneScrollbackInfo> {
     registry
         .entries()
